Bioinformatics_Analyisis

Jorlen Moreno

2026-04-27

Load the library

library(seqinr)

Read the fasta files

austria_data <- read.fasta("austria.fasta")
brazil_data <- read.fasta("brazil.fasta")
india_data <- read.fasta("india.fasta")

seq_austria <- austria_data[[1]]
seq_brazil  <- brazil_data[[1]]
seq_india   <- india_data[[1]]

We make a list out of the datasets

seqs_list <- list(
  Austria = seq_austria,
  Brazil  = seq_brazil,
  India   = seq_india
)
seqs_list
## $Austria

## [29647] "a" "g" "c" "c" "a" "c" "c" "a" "c" "a" "t" "t" "t" "t" "c" "a" "c" "c"
## [29665] "g" "a" "g" "g" "c" "c" "a" "c" "g" "c" "g" "g" "a" "g" "t" "a" "c" "g"
## [29683] "a" "t" "c" "g" "a" "g" "t" "g" "t" "a" "c" "a" "c" "t" "c" "a" "a" "c"
## [29701] "a" "a" "t" "g" "c" "t" "a" "g" "g" "g" "a" "g" "a" "g" "c" "t" "g" "c"
## [29719] "c" "t" "a" "t" "a" "t" "g" "g" "a" "a" "g" "a" "g" "c" "c" "c" "t" "a"
## [29737] "a" "t" "g" "t" "g" "t" "a" "a" "a" "a" "t" "t" "a" "a" "t" "t" "t" "t"
## [29755] "a" "g" "t" "a" "g" "t" "g" "c" "t" "a" "t" "c" "c" "c" "c" "a" "t" "g"
## [29773] "t" "g" "a" "t" "t" "t" "t" "a" "a" "t" "a"
## attr(,"name")
## [1] "MZ413358.1"
## attr(,"Annot")
## [1] ">MZ413358.1 Severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 isolate SARS-CoV-2/human/IND/GBRC813/2021, complete genome"
## attr(,"class")
## [1] "SeqFastadna"

Then, we will calculate the length of each sequence and will graph it.

lengths <- sapply(seqs_list, length)

print("Sequence longitudes")
## [1] "Sequence longitudes"
print(lengths)
## Austria  Brazil   India 
##   29763   29827   29783
#we create the plot for each one
barplot(lengths,
        main = "Viral sequences lengths",
        col = hcl.colors(4, "Plasma"),
        ylab = "Nucleotides",
        xlab = "Origin$")

Then we visualize nucleotide composition.

comp_nucleotides <- sapply(seqs_list, function(x) count(x, 1))

# Barplot to count ACGT nucleotides
barplot(comp_nucleotides,
        beside = TRUE, 
        main = "Nucleotide composition by origin",
        col = hcl.colors(4, "Plasma"),
        legend = rownames(comp_nucleotides),
        xlab = "Origin",
        ylab = "Frequence")

Get the GC percentage

gc_percentage <- sapply(seqs_list, GC) * 100

print("GC content:")
## [1] "GC content:"
print(gc_percentage)
##  Austria   Brazil    India 
## 37.97332 37.99578 37.97468
#gc barplot
barplot(gc_percentage,
        main = "GC content (%)",
        col = hcl.colors(4, "Plasma"),
        ylab = "% GC",
        xlab = "Origin",
        ylim = c(0, 60))

Finally, we get the complimentray sequence

comp_austria <- c2s(comp(seq_austria))
comp_brazil  <- c2s(comp(seq_brazil))
comp_india   <- c2s(comp(seq_india))

print("complementary sequences:")
## [1] "complementary sequences:"
print(substr(comp_austria, 1, 50))
## [1] "tctagacaagagatttgcttgaaattttagacacaccgacagtgagccga"
print(substr(comp_brazil, 1, 50))
## [1] "tggttggttgaaagctagagaacatctagacaagagatttgcttgaaatt"
print(substr(comp_india, 1, 50))
## [1] "aacatctagacaagagatttgcttgaaattttagacacaccgacagtgag"
